summary: Smoke-tested SBI Financial Co-Pilot backend + frontend. Login flow, dashboard, transactions, financial-health, recommendations, analytics, wealth-simulator, AI chat, OTP send/verify and chat-history all work via public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. One CRITICAL backend bug: POST /api/auth/register returns 500 (ObjectId serialization). Frontend register flow is therefore blocked. All other UI pages render correctly with seeded data (123 transactions, charts, sliders, health gauge).
passed: POST /api/transactions creates tx (requires transaction_type, transaction_date, merchant_name)
POST /api/ai/chat with {question} returns Gemini answer using user context
GET /api/analytics/monthly-spending, category-breakdown, savings-trend all return data
Frontend: Landing page loads (title: SBI Financial Co-Pilot)
Frontend: Login flow -> redirects to /dashboard with 'Welcome back!' toast
Frontend: Dashboard renders Balance/Income/Expenses/Potential Savings cards, savings chart, health score gauge (21/100)
Frontend: Transactions page shows 123 rows with date/merchant/category/method/amount + Upload CSV button
Frontend: AI Co-Pilot Chat page renders, prior Gemini responses visible
Frontend: Wealth Simulator renders with 3 sliders (investment/duration/return) and Project button
Frontend: Sidebar nav with Dashboard/Transactions/Financial Health/AI Co-Pilot/Wealth/Recommendations/Analytics

failed.issue: POST /api/auth/register returns 500 Internal Server Error. Backend log: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]. FastAPI is trying to serialize a Mongo document containing _id (ObjectId) directly. This blocks the entire registration / 3-step KYC onboarding flow on the frontend.
failed.evidence: curl POST /api/auth/register with {email,password,full_name,phone} -> {"detail":"Internal server error"}; supervisor backend.err.log shows ObjectId not iterable traceback in pydantic response serialization.

action_items: Fix POST /api/auth/register response serialization: ensure the returned user/token payload does not include raw Mongo _id (ObjectId). Either strip _id before returning, project to a Pydantic response_model with id:str (uuid), or jsonable_encoder with custom encoder for ObjectId.
After fix, re-run the 3-step frontend register flow (details -> OTP -> KYC) to verify auto-login and that ~70-90 seeded transactions land for the new user.
Note (non-blocker): POST /api/transactions schema uses transaction_type, transaction_date, merchant_name (not type/date). Confirm frontend create-transaction form sends these field names.
Note (non-blocker): POST /api/ai/chat expects field `question` (not `message`). Confirm frontend payload matches.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (register flow not exercised due to backend 500)
